channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Legally blind Ohio artist says she can't drive, but she can tattoo
item.description: Nicole Morrone was born legally blind, but she still creates detailed, colorful artwork -- on her clients' skin.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cloutier, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican nominee for New Jersey governor puts some distance from Trump on immigration
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — At a Spanish-speaking town hall, the Republican candidate for New Jersey governor assured two young Mexican immigrants who were brought to the United States as children that people without criminal records would not face deportation.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months, Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massachusetts school staffer dies after being assaulted by student
item.description: The student, who has not been identified, has been charged with assault and battery causing serious bodily injury.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Routine school day turns tragic as staffer dies after student allegedly boots her in chest: DA
item.description: Staff member Amy Morrell died after a 14-year-old student allegedly kicked her in the chest during an attempted departure from the dorm at a Massachusetts therapeutic school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Community rallies around beloved therapy dog diagnosed with cancer
item.description: "Jay is very Zen, mellow and lovely," Christie said. "Kids made him a bunch of birthday cards and families love saying hi to him daily.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, NewsNation

item.title: Peak of Orionid meteor shower nears: How and when to watch
item.description: If the supermoon wasn't enough, October is treating skygazers to another celestial delight – the peak of the Orionid meteor shower.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: South Carolina schedules execution for man who taunted police with message in victim's blood
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina inmate who killed a man, burned his eyes with cigarettes and then painted “catch me if u can” on the wall with the victim's blood more than 20 years ago has been scheduled to be executed next month.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione’s lawyers say Trump admin is making him political pawn
item.description: Luigi Mangione's defense team issued a letter to the judge arguing the Trump admin is using him as a political pawn to further its agenda.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: What is the 'AI homeless man prank'? Police says it's dangerous
item.description: An AI-driven TikTok trend is resulting in 911 calls by panicked people who think a man has broken into their homes.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Daniel Cameron banking on his name ID to overcome lackluster fundraising in Kentucky Senate race
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Making his pitch to connect with a Republican crowd that instantly recognized him, Kentucky Senate candidate Daniel Cameron likened the gathering to a family reunion — a feel-good reminder of his status as a party stalwart in the Bluegrass State.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE arrests Illinois officer with expired visa
item.description: He overstayed a B-2 tourist visa that required him to leave the country by March 31, 2015, according to ICE officials.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KRON4

item.title: GOP senators worry about Trump, Hegseth shutdown moves
item.description: Republican senators have questions and concerns about how President Donald Trump is managing to pay more than 1 million military service members during the government shutdown and are seeking more information about what funds he is dipping into to achieve his political ends.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Homeland Security says Marine's father who was deported had faced domestic violence, assault charges
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The father of a Marine who was arrested by immigration authorities when visiting his pregnant daughter at Camp Pendleton has a criminal record that includes charges of domestic violence and aggravated assault with a deadly weapon, the Department of Homeland Security said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil can freely travel around US as he fights his deportation case, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has lifted travel restrictions for Mahmoud Khalil, allowing the Palestinian activist to speak at rallies and other events across the U.S. as he fights his deportation case brought by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials release cause of death for newlywed couple found in car just days before anniversary
item.description: Illinois officials have released a cause of death for a newlywed couple that was found dead in their car on Oct. 6, but an investigation is still ongoing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family members share Diane Keaton's cause of death
item.description: Family members of legendary actress Diane Keaton, who passed away Saturday at the age of 79, revealed the Oscar-winning actress died during a battle with pneumonia, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McConnell 'fine' after falling in Senate office building, spokesperson says
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) fell on Thursday in front of left-wing protesters while walking through a Senate office building.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KRON4

item.title: Blue state governor vows 'zero tolerance' approach to street takeovers after violent attack on law enforcement
item.description: Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey announced the results of a crackdown on illegal street takeovers by the state police, saying seven new arrests have been made.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PA system takeover at Pennsylvania airport is 'wake-up call,' security expert says
item.description: An incident where an unauthorized user gained access to Harrisburg International Airport's public address system could have been much worse, a security expert said.
item.dc:creator: Harry Murphy, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Israel identifies remains of 2 more hostages
item.description: Israel’s National Institute of Forensic Medicine on Thursday identified the remains of two more hostages returned from Gaza, as officials and families warned Hamas to hand over the bodies of those still held.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Colleges are fighting to prove their return on investment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For a generation of young Americans, choosing where to go to college — or whether to go at all — has become a complex calculation of costs and benefits that often revolves around a single question: Is the degree worth its price? Public confidence in higher education has plummeted i
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NHC watching Caribbean for potential tropical development
item.description: The National Hurricane Center is watching the Caribbean Sea for potential tropical development next week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida teen arrested after shooting himself in staged abduction, deputies say
item.description: The 17-year-old who staged his own abduction and caused a statewide AMBER Alert has been arrested, Florida deputies say.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: Relatives of Salvadoran family slain in Georgia crash left reeling from tragedy
item.description: TEPETITAN, El Salvador (AP) — Parents, siblings, aunts, nephews and other relatives of a Salvadoran family of eight who died in the fiery crash of a semitrailer and a van in Georgia this week struggled to wrap their minds around the tragedy on Wednesday as they hoped that the bodies of their lost lo
item.dc:creator: MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capitol Police called to investigate US flag with swastika in GOP congressional office
item.description: Rep. Dave Taylor (R-Ohio) on Wednesday condemned a symbol on the wall of his Washington, D.C., office, which was reportedly a swastika in the middle of a U.S. flag.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WGN 9

item.title: Security experts warn of increased cyber risk after end of Windows 10 support
item.description: Cyber security experts are warning Windows 10 users of an increased risk of malicious activity after support officially ended Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, KRON4

item.title: Journalists turn in access badges, exit Pentagon rather than agree to new reporting rules
item.description: Dozens of reporters turned in access badges and exited the Pentagon on Wednesday rather than agree to government-imposed restrictions on their work, pushing journalists who cover the American military further from the seat of its power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dunkin' launches new Halloween-themed menu item
item.description: A new drink, the return of a fan-favorite, and a bucket? You can find them all at Dunkin' starting today.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missouri man executed for the fatal shooting of a state trooper in 2005
item.description: BONNE TERRE, Mo. (AP) — A Missouri man who long maintained his innocence was executed Tuesday for the fatal shooting of a state trooper more than 20 years ago. Lance Shockley, 48, was pronounced dead at 6:13 p.m. following a lethal injection at the state prison in Bonne Terre.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump honors 'martyr for truth' Charlie Kirk with Medal of Freedom
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday awarded the nation’s top civilian honor posthumously to conservative activist Charlie Kirk, describing him as a “martyr for truth and for freedom.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania man pleads guilty in arson attack at governor’s mansion while Shapiro’s family slept
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and used beer bottles filled with gasoline to ignite the occupied Pennsylvania governor’s mansion pleaded guilty Tuesday to the attempted murder of Gov. Josh Shapiro.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows illegal BASE jumping in Yosemite amid government shutdown
item.description: The lack of staffing has led some to engage in dangerous and illegal activity, with videos circulating on social media showing people BASE jumping.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, Fox 5

item.title: 4 arrests made in connection with mass shooting that left 6 dead in Mississippi, FBI says
item.description: Three people have been arrested on murder charges and a fourth person on an attempted murder charge in a weekend shooting that left six dead and more than a dozen injured in a small Mississippi town, the FBI announced Monday. Teviyon L.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: After hostages and prisoners freed, complex issues remain for Israel-Hamas
item.description: Issues such as whether Hamas will disarm and who will govern Gaza — and the question of Palestinian statehood — remain unresolved, highlighting the fragility of an agreement that for now only pauses the deadliest conflict in the history of Israel and the Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Fox 5
